clinical_criteria: Birthing Person opioid exposure record from 30 to 2 days prior to delivery
Birthing Person opioid use disorder diagnosis from prenatal through 30-days postpartum
Birthing Person positive opioid toxicology during birth hospitalization
algorithm_summary: The phenotype algorithm identifies opioid-exposed infants using a population of birthing person-infant dyads from 2010-2022. Population criteria include: evidence of live birth, infant >=33 weeks gestation, and exclusion of critical illness/respiratory procedures and major congenital malformations. Opioid exposure is identified through 6 indicators: (1) infant NOWS/opioid-exposure diagnosis, (2) infant positive opioid toxicology, (3) birthing person opioid use disorder diagnosis (prenatal through 30-days postpartum), (4) birthing person outpatient opioid exposure record (30 to 2 days prior to delivery), (5) birthing person opioid in medication list (30 to 2 days prior to delivery), or (6) birthing person positive opioid toxicology. The algorithm achieved high positive predictive values (95.4-100%) when validated against medical record review. Various combinations of these 6 indicators can be used depending on study requirements.
